title: sorghum vulgare var. technicum
intro: Sorghum vulgare var. technicum, also known as broomcorn, is a variant of the sorghum plant, a type of grain native to Africa and Asia, characterized by its sturdy stalks and wide panicles. Traditionally, sorghum vulgare var. technicum has been used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of sorghum vulgare var. technicum often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
